short: A speed-up that lets a model guess several next words from its own internal signals, no helper model.
definition: EAGLE is a speculative decoding method, a way of speeding up generation by guessing several words ahead and checking them at once. It adds small predictor components to a model that draft candidate words directly from the model's own internal signals, removing the need to load and run a separate, smaller helper model. A verification step then accepts or rejects each guess, speeding up generation while keeping the output identical to normal.
